name: AI Content Authenticity & Detection Tools
description: The proliferation of AI-generated content and images creates a critical need for reliable detection tools, yet current solutions are prone to errors (false positives, difficulty identifying subtle manipulations). Users and institutions struggle to discern authentic human-created content from AI-produced material, leading to frustration and real-world harm.
pain_points: Unreliable AI detection tools with high false positives.
Struggle to distinguish human vs. AI-generated content.
Lack of transparency and explainability in current AI detectors.
Erosion of trust in online content authenticity.
Real-world harm from misidentified AI content (e.g., academic integrity).
tech_stack: Custom Microcontroller with Secure Enclave (e.g., ARM TrustZone compatible)
Biometric/Haptic Sensors (integrated into device for input capture)
Cryptographic Libraries (e.g., OpenSSL, Libsodium for hashing/signing)
Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT) or Blockchain (e.g., Hyperledger Fabric for public verification records)
Hardware Design & Manufacturing (PCBA, secure enclosure design)
